issuesFound.title: Page title is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. Users relying on screen readers or browser tabs cannot identify the page content or purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Button missing accessible name and aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button contains only an icon with aria-hidden="true", providing no accessible name. It also controls a menu but lacks aria-expanded to indicate state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 33-36: <button
  type="button"
  class="nav-toggle"
  aria-label="Toggle navigation menu"
  aria-expanded="false"
  aria-controls="nav-menu"
>

issuesFound.title: Submit button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search form submit button contains an image with alt="" and aria-hidden="true", resulting in no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 73: <button type="submit" aria-label="Search">

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The "Solutions" button toggles a submenu but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 43-46: <button
  type="button"
  aria-expanded="false"
  aria-controls="solutions-submenu"
>
  Solutions
</button>

issuesFound.title: Link text is non-descriptive
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" does not describe the destination without surrounding context. Screen reader users navigating by links cannot distinguish this from other "Read more" links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 109-111: Start free trial (no credit card required)

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hero dashboard image lacks an alt attribute, making it inaccessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 118-121:

issuesFound.title: Form input missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input has no associated <label>, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Placeholder text is not a sufficient label.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 503-509: <input
  type="email"
  id="newsletter-email"
  name="email"
  autocomplete="email"
  required
  aria-required="true"
  placeholder="you@example.com"
  aria-label="Email address for newsletter"
>

issuesFound.title: Personal data input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The first name input collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete attribute, hindering users who rely on browser autofill.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 529-534: <input
  type="text"
  id="contact-first-name"
  name="first_name"
  autocomplete="given-name"
  required
>

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The table header "Feature" lacks a scope attribute in a table with multiple column headers, making the axis ambiguous for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 369: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.title: Landmark region missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <section> elements on the page. This hero section lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby to distinguish it from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100: <section class="hero" aria-label="Hero introduction">

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <nav> elements on the page. The main navigation lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 30: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The section references aria-labelledby="features-heading", but no element with id="features-heading" exists in the document.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 153:

Everything you need to ship accessible software

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy jumps from h2 (Line 182) to h5 (Line 192) without intermediate h3 or h4 levels, confusing users navigating by headings.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 192:

Connect your project

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring (box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline) for keyboard users. This makes it impossible to identify the focused element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 434: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border, hiding focus state from keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 583: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link focus style removes the outline without a visible replacement, preventing keyboard users from seeing focus on the header logo.
issuesFound.fix: Line 657: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 683: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has a width and height of 28px with no padding, which is below the 44px minimum recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 765: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has a width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1388: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px with no compensating padding, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1408: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; padding: 8px 0; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, making content fully visible instead of hiding it visually for screen readers only.
issuesFound.fix: Line 456: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transform and transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at this line is empty and does not disable the motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 523: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element uses transform and transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at this line has empty rules and does not disable the motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 893: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #555555 / background #ffffff ratio 3.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The body text colour (#555555 derived from --colour-neutral-900) on the page background (#ffffff) has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.3:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 19: --colour-neutral-900: #333333;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration (underlines), relying solely on colour to distinguish links from text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive colour.
issuesFound.fix: Line 378: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now open.

  Line 1367:   setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

  Line 1397:   setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not set initial aria-expanded state

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without passing the required aria-label prop. Screen reader users will hear "button" without knowing its purpose (switching themes).
issuesFound.fix: Line 577: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-pressed state (Monthly)
issuesFound.explanation: The BillingToggle monthly button tracks selection state via the value prop but lacks aria-pressed. Assistive technology users cannot identify the selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 893: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={[...]} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}>

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-pressed state (Annual)
issuesFound.explanation: The BillingToggle annual button tracks selection state via the value prop but lacks aria-pressed. Assistive technology users cannot identify the selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 900: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={[...]} onClick={() => onChange('annual')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}>

issuesFound.title: Disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FaqItemComponent button toggles the visibility of the answer panel but does not have aria-expanded to reflect the isOpen state. Screen reader users are not informed whether the content is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1107: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: Card article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard article element does not have an aria-labelledby attribute referencing the heading inside it (id={headingId}). Screen reader users navigating by landmarks or articles will encounter an unnamed region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 937: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: Card primary CTA missing descriptive accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard CTA link text (e.g., "Start Pro free trial") may be ambiguous when read out of context in a list of links. It should include the plan name for clarity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 972: <a href={plan.ctaHref} className={[...]} aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} — ${plan.name}`}>

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name (Carousel Prev)
issuesFound.explanation: The TestimonialsCarousel previous button is an IconButton that does not receive an aria-label prop. Screen reader users will not know it navigates to the previous testimonial.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1074: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev" aria-label="Previous testimonial">

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name (Carousel Next)
issuesFound.explanation: The TestimonialsCarousel next button is an IconButton that does not receive an aria-label prop. Screen reader users will not know it navigates to the next testimonial.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1079: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next" aria-label="Next testimonial">

issuesFound.title: Star rating decorative icons missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component renders individual star spans that are decorative within the labelled container. They lack aria-hidden="true", potentially causing screen readers to announce them redundantly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 214: <span key={i} aria-hidden="true" className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'}>

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component sets aria-disabled when isLoading is true, but does not set aria-busy. aria-busy is the correct state to indicate content is loading while the element remains in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 229: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? 'true' : undefined} className={[...]} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Required field marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component renders a visual asterisk for required fields without aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce "star" or "asterisk" unnecessarily.
issuesFound.fix: Line 299: *

issuesFound.title: Repeated landmark region missing accessible name (SkipLinks)
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple navs on the page (Header, Mobile, Footer), this nav needs a unique name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 337: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.fix: Line 435: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Repeated landmark region missing accessible name (MobileNav)
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav component renders an inner <nav> element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple navs on the page, this nav needs a unique name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 507: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: Section landmark missing accessible name (Hero)
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection renders a <section> without aria-labelledby or aria-label. It contains an h1 with id="hero-heading" which should be referenced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Section landmark missing accessible name (Stats)

issuesFound.fix: Line 717: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">
